text.title: Rust Testing Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns: missing layers, ignored doctests, weak proptest configuration, test-isolation leaks.

antipattern.symptom: Crate has `#[cfg(test)] mod tests` everywhere but no `tests/*.rs` and no `///` examples. Refactor breaks downstream silently.
antipattern.root-cause: Author treats unit tests as enough because they ran in the same crate. Integration tests catch a different class — they exercise the public surface as a consumer would.
antipattern.fix: Add at least one file under `tests/` exercising the public API. Add `# Examples` sections to every public `///` doc block.

antipattern.symptom: Documentation contains code fences marked ```` ```rust,ignore ```` or ```` ```rust,no_run ```` without justification. CI passes; broken examples shipped to docs.rs.
antipattern.root-cause: `ignore` and `no_run` are escape hatches. Used unjustifiedly they hide rot.
antipattern.fix: Replace `ignore` with `no_run` only when example needs unavailable resources (DB, network). Replace `no_run` with full execution when feasible. Lint: grep for `rust,ignore` requiring justifying comment.

antipattern.symptom: `proptest!` block uses `prop_assert!` and shrinks; but the strategy generator uses `any::<String>()` producing 10MB strings, slowing CI to 30s+ per case.
antipattern.root-cause: Default `any::<String>` has no size limit. Without `prop::collection::vec(any::<u8>(), 0..100)` style bounds, tests run on adversarial pathological inputs.
antipattern.fix: Size-bound every generator: `prop::string::string_regex("[a-z]{0,16}")`, `prop::collection::vec(_, 0..32)`. Set `proptest_cases` to 256 for fast feedback, 4096 for nightly.

antipattern.symptom: Tests pass when run individually, fail under `cargo test --jobs 1` or in CI. Diagnosis: shared `static mut`, file-system writes to `/tmp/fixed-path`, or env-var setters.

root-cause.source: Rust Book ch.11; cargo test parallelism

antipattern.root-cause: Cargo runs tests in parallel threads by default. Shared global mutable state or fixed filesystem paths produce flakes.
antipattern.fix: Use `tempfile::TempDir` for per-test directories. Use `static once_cell::sync::Lazy` + interior mutability for genuinely shared resources. Document `#[serial]` (via `serial_test`) when serialisation is required.
